steps.title: Use the reanalysis
steps.content: The first study proposed that red uniforms helped Manchester United win by causing fear and hesitation. Ben-Ami and colleagues then found no evidence that red-uniformed teams are more likely than other teams to win.

steps.title: Reject red as the cause
steps.content: Choice B repeats the red-uniform explanation that the reanalysis undermines. Choice C introduces player satisfaction with no support. Choice D shifts to other teams' uniform decisions. If red provided no winning advantage, Manchester United's home performance likely did not depend on wearing red. The answer is A.
